Tests the behavior of Math.min function when one of the arguments is NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses the `assert` function to verify the behavior of the `Math.min` function when one of its arguments is `NaN`. The script checks that the result of `Math.min(5, 7, NaN)` is `NaN`, which confirms that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when any of its arguments is `NaN`. This test ensures that the `Math.min` function adheres to the expected behavior as defined by the ECMAScript specification. The code provides narrow functionality focused on validating a specific aspect of the `Math.min` function.
